old_claim.note: GPS-Guided Logistics supports manufacturing, deployment, commercialization, or operational scaling.

new_claim_absent: No direct GPS-guided logistics dependency remains because fleet-route optimization was formulated as an operations-research problem in 1959, decades before commercial GPS fleet systems.

source_shape.source_locator: The Truck Dispatching Problem, abstract and pp. 80-91: formulates fleet routes from terminals, truck capacities, customer demands, and distances, with hand or digital-computer calculation in 1959.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The foundational paper demonstrates route-optimization methods using operations-research variables and computation before GPS-guided fleet logistics existed.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: GPS can supply useful live position data to later routing systems, but the optimization method and its historical milestone do not require that downstream telemetry workflow.

ontology_before: Route optimization was modeled as commercially dependent on the later GPS-guided fleet logistics application.
ontology_after: Route optimization retains its operations-research and software lineage without a backwards dependency on GPS fleet operations.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-gps-navstar-logistics-boundary: route_optimization_logistics has no direct gps_guided_logistics prerequisite while operations_research remains present.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped exclusively to dynamic GPS-fed vehicle routing rather than the general fleet-route optimization method.
Primary evidence shows the 1959 truck-dispatch method required satellite position telemetry.
short_reason: Route optimization was formally demonstrated before GPS fleet logistics and does not require it.
